title: The Weeping Icon
narrative: An icon of the Theotokos stands in the hallway. Myrrh flows from her eyes. You do not know why. You stand before her and feel that she is looking at you specifically.

choices.text: Kneel and pray
Ask her intercession for the fallen
Touch the icon with reverence

title: The Pantocrator
narrative: Christ as ruler of all, right hand raised in blessing. The left holds the Gospels open to a page you cannot read from here. What arrests you is the eyes — not judging, not forgiving. Witnessing.

choices.text: Stand under his gaze without flinching
Bow and do not look up
Ask him what the open page says

title: The Descent into Hades
narrative: Christ stands on the shattered gates of Hades, reaching into the tomb to pull Adam up by the wrist. Adam is not climbing. He is being lifted. The icon makes no room for self-rescue.

choices.text: Extend your hand as Adam does
Ask what it cost him to descend
Kneel at the broken gates

title: The Three Holy Youths
narrative: Shadrach, Meshach, Abednego stand in the fire. A fourth figure is with them. The icon does not explain who the fourth is. The fire is still fire. They are simply not burning.

choices.text: Step toward the fire
Pray the names of those in the flames
Ask who the fourth figure is

title: The Holy Trinity
narrative: Three angels at a table. An open space at the front. Rublev painted it so that the viewer enters the composition — you are not looking at the Trinity, you are being invited in. The empty seat has always been for you.

choices.text: Sit in the open seat
Remain standing in reverence
Ask what being invited means

title: The Ladder of Divine Ascent
narrative: Monks climb a ladder toward Christ at the top. Demons hang from the rungs pulling them off. Some fall. One, near the top, looks down. The Church Fathers said this was the most dangerous moment — when you can see how far you have come.

choices.text: Look up, not down
Climb without counting rungs
Reach back for the monk behind you

title: The Transfiguration
narrative: Christ blazes white on Tabor. Moses and Elijah flank him. Peter, James, and John have fallen to the ground — not in prayer but in overwhelm, hands over their faces. The light is not metaphorical. The fathers said it was increated, the same light given to the saints in theosis.

choices.text: Shield your eyes and remain standing
Fall to the ground as the disciples did
Open your eyes to the light

title: The Nativity
narrative: The cave is dark. The star illuminates it from above. Mary lies exhausted, turned away from the child — not abandonment, the fathers wrote, but contemplation. Joseph sits apart, head in his hand, wrestling with something the icon does not name.

choices.text: Kneel at the manger in the dark
Sit with Joseph in his struggle
Trace the path of the star

title: The Dormition
narrative: The Theotokos lies on her deathbed. The apostles crowd around her. Christ stands at the center holding an infant — her soul, wrapped and radiant. She died as she lived: in surrender. The icon renders death not as ending but as return.

choices.text: Stand with the apostles in vigil
Look at what Christ holds
Pray the Kontakion of Dormition

title: The Last Judgment
narrative: The scroll of deeds unrolls across the top of the image. The sheep to the right. The goats to the left. The river of fire runs through the middle. What strikes you is not the fire — it is that the same Christ who feeds the sheep is the same Christ the goats would not recognize.

choices.text: Ask to stand with the merciful
Examine your deeds against the scroll
Pray for those in the fire
